summary: Tune CloudFront delivery cost and performance — price class, compression, default TTL — plus tag housekeeping, a guardrailed alias addition, and two engineer-gated operations (WAF association and distribution deletion) that carry security or destructive blast radius.

operations.title: Change price class
operations.summary: Change which edge locations serve this distribution - fewer regions cost less but can add latency outside them.
operations.description: Changes which set of CloudFront edge locations serve this distribution; narrower price classes cost less but serve fewer regions, which can add latency for viewers outside them.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: new_price_class
params.label: New price class
params.help: PriceClass_100 = US/Canada/Europe only; PriceClass_200 adds Asia/Africa/Middle East; PriceClass_All covers every edge location.

operations.title: Toggle automatic compression
operations.summary: Turn automatic compression on or off; turning it off can increase file sizes served to viewers.
operations.description: Turns CloudFront's automatic gzip/brotli compression on or off for the default cache behavior; disabling it increases payload size for compressible content but can help with origins that already compress responses.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: compress
params.label: Enable compression
params.help: Whether CloudFront compresses eligible file types for this behavior.

operations.title: Change default cache TTL
operations.summary: Change how long content stays cached at the edge before CloudFront rechecks the origin server.
operations.description: Adjusts how long, in seconds, CloudFront caches objects at the edge before re-checking the origin; lowering it freshens content faster at the cost of more origin requests, raising it reduces origin load but can serve stale content longer.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: new_default_ttl
params.label: Default TTL (seconds)
params.help: Default object lifetime in the CloudFront cache. Origin cache-control headers can still override this per-object.

operations.title: Add a tag
operations.summary: Add a tag for cost tracking or ownership. Metadata only - no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Adds a single key/value tag to the distribution for cost allocation, ownership, or automation purposes; existing tags are left untouched.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key, e.g. cost-center or environment.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Value to store under the tag key.

operations.title: Add an alternate domain name (CNAME)
operations.summary: Add an alternate domain name to this distribution; it must already be covered by the certificate or HTTPS will fail.
operations.description: Adds an alternate domain name (CNAME/alias) that viewers can use to reach this distribution. The domain must already be covered by the distribution's ACM certificate SANs or viewer requests will fail TLS validation.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to add the alias to.

params.name: new_alias
params.label: Alternate domain name

bounds.semantic: must already be covered by the distribution's ACM certificate SANs

params.help: Domain to add as a CNAME, e.g. app.example.com. Confirm the ACM certificate on this distribution covers it before submitting.

operations.title: Associate or change WAFv2 Web ACL
operations.summary: Attach or swap the firewall protecting this distribution; an engineer reviews it first since bad rules can block real traffic.
operations.description: Associates a global (CLOUDFRONT-scope) WAFv2 Web ACL with the distribution, or swaps the currently associated one. Misconfigured or overly strict ACL rules can block legitimate traffic, so this is authored and reviewed by an engineer.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution whose WAF association is changing.

params.name: web_acl_arn
params.label: WAFv2 Web ACL ARN

bounds.semantic: must be a CLOUDFRONT-scope (global, us-east-1) WAFv2 Web ACL

params.help: ARN of the global WAFv2 Web ACL to attach. Leave the field empty in a follow-up change request to remove WAF protection.

operations.title: Delete a distribution
operations.summary: Permanently delete this distribution; an engineer reviews it first. This can't be undone, and content stops serving once it's removed.
operations.description: Permanently removes the CloudFront distribution. AWS requires the distribution to be disabled and fully propagated (typically 15-20 minutes) before it can be destroyed, and once removed the CDN endpoint, cached content, and any dependent CNAMEs stop resolving for viewers.

params.name: distribution
params.label: CloudFront distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to permanently delete. It must be disabled first.

operations.title: Toggle IPv6
operations.summary: Turn IPv6 access on or off for viewers reaching this distribution. Applies in place with no downtime.
operations.description: Enable or disable is_ipv6_enabled on the distribution. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution to update, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; toggling IPv6 is an in-place update with no downtime.

params.name: is_ipv6_enabled
params.label: IPv6 enabled
params.help: Controls whether viewers can reach the distribution over IPv6 in addition to IPv4 (true) or over IPv4 only (false); the change applies in place with no downtime and is fully reversible, so both allowed values are safe.

operations.title: Change maximum HTTP version
operations.summary: Set the highest HTTP version this distribution offers viewers; older clients still connect using an earlier version.
operations.description: Set http_version (max HTTP version negotiated with viewers). Bounded enum, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose maximum viewer HTTP version is being changed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted.

params.name: http_version
params.label: HTTP version
params.help: The highest HTTP version CloudFront will negotiate with viewers (http1.1 up to http2and3/http3); clients that do not support the chosen maximum automatically use an older version, so every value in this bounded list keeps existing viewers working.

operations.title: Set default root object
operations.summary: Set which file this distribution serves when a viewer requests the root URL, such as index.html.
operations.description: Set default_root_object (the object CloudFront returns for a request to the root URL, e.g. index.html). In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose default root object is being set, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; the change is in-place and reversible.

params.name: default_root_object
params.label: Default root object
params.help: Object path CloudFront serves at the root, e.g. index.html. No leading slash; up to 255 chars.

operations.title: Edit distribution comment
operations.summary: Edit this distribution's description. Cosmetic only - it has no effect on how traffic is served.
operations.description: Set the free-text comment/description (max 128 chars). Cosmetic, in-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose comment is being edited, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; the comment is cosmetic metadata with no effect on traffic.

params.name: comment
params.label: Comment
params.help: Human-readable description, up to 128 characters.

operations.title: Toggle Smooth Streaming
operations.summary: Turn support for Microsoft Smooth Streaming media on or off for this distribution's default caching behavior.
operations.description: Enable or disable smooth_streaming on the default cache behavior (Microsoft Smooth Streaming format). In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose default cache behavior is being toggled, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; the change is in-place with no downtime.

params.name: smooth_streaming
params.label: Smooth Streaming
params.help: Controls whether the default cache behavior distributes media files in Microsoft Smooth Streaming format (true) or not (false); it only matters when the origin serves that media type, and the in-place, reversible toggle makes both allowed values safe.

operations.title: Change default behavior max TTL
operations.summary: Set the longest time this distribution's default behavior can cache an object before rechecking the origin.
operations.description: Set max_ttl (seconds) on the default cache behavior. Bounded 0 to 31536000 (1 year). In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose default-behavior maximum cache TTL is being changed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted.

params.name: max_ttl
params.label: Max TTL (seconds)
params.help: Maximum time in seconds objects stay in the CloudFront cache. 0 to 31536000 (1 year).

operations.title: Change default behavior min TTL
operations.summary: Set the shortest time this distribution's default behavior must cache an object before rechecking the origin.
operations.description: Set min_ttl (seconds) on the default cache behavior. Bounded 0 to 31536000 (1 year). In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose default-behavior minimum cache TTL is being changed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted.

params.name: min_ttl
params.label: Min TTL (seconds)
params.help: Minimum time in seconds objects stay in the CloudFront cache. 0 to 31536000 (1 year).

operations.title: Toggle cookie logging
operations.summary: Turn cookie logging on or off in this distribution's access logs; only what's recorded changes, never live traffic.
operations.description: Enable or disable include_cookies on logging_config (whether cookies are written to access logs). In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose access-log cookie setting is being changed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; only logging output is affected, never live traffic.

params.name: include_cookies
params.label: Log cookies
params.help: Controls whether viewer cookies are written into the distribution's access logs (true) or omitted (false); the setting only changes what appears in logs — note that logging cookies can capture session data, while serving of live traffic is never affected either way.

operations.title: Remove a tag
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this distribution. Metadata only - you can always add it back.
operations.description: Remove a single key from the tags map on the distribution. In-place, reversible (re-add), no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution to remove the tag from, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; tags are metadata only and a removed key can be re-added.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: The tag key to remove from this distribution.

operations.title: Change viewer protocol policy
operations.summary: Set whether viewers must use HTTPS or may also use plain HTTP; allowing plain HTTP is the less secure choice.
operations.description: Set viewer_protocol_policy on the default cache behavior. Bounded enum but security-relevant (allow-all permits plain HTTP), so guardrailed. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution whose viewer protocol policy is being changed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; the change is in-place but guardrailed because allow-all permits insecure HTTP.

params.name: viewer_protocol_policy
params.label: Viewer protocol policy
params.help: allow-all permits insecure HTTP; redirect-to-https or https-only are the secure choices.

operations.title: Enable or disable the distribution
operations.summary: Turn this distribution on or off. Disabling it takes the site offline until it's re-enabled.
operations.description: Set enabled on the distribution. Disabling stops CloudFront from serving content (site goes offline) so it is guardrailed. In-place, reversible.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The existing CloudFront distribution to enable or disable, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed distribution can be targeted; disabling stops it serving all content until re-enabled, which is why this toggle is guardrailed.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Enabled
params.help: Setting false stops the distribution from serving content until re-enabled.

operations.title: Request another change to CloudFront distribution
operations.summary: Request any other change to this distribution; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change to a CloudFront distribution not offered as a bounded form. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform before apply.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: The distribution to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle wait-for-deployment
operations.summary: Choose whether applying this change waits for CloudFront to finish deploying, or returns right away. Viewers see no difference.
operations.description: Set the `wait_for_deployment` boolean on the distribution. This controls ONLY whether Terraform blocks during apply until the distribution status changes from InProgress to Deployed; it has no effect on the live distribution's serving configuration. Provider default is true.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: wait_for_deployment
params.label: Wait for deployment
params.help: true = Terraform waits for CloudFront propagation to finish; false = apply returns immediately. No effect on served content.

operations.title: Toggle mTLS trust-store CA-name advertisement
operations.summary: Choose whether this distribution hints its trusted certificate authorities to mutual-TLS viewers; accepted certificates don't change.
operations.description: Set `viewer_mtls_config.trust_store_config.advertise_trust_store_ca_names`, a viewer-mTLS discoverability aid that controls whether CloudFront advertises the trust store's CA names to clients during the TLS handshake. It does not change which client certificates are accepted. Provider default is false. Applies only when viewer mTLS is already configured on the distribution.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: advertise_trust_store_ca_names
params.label: Advertise trust-store CA names
params.help: Discoverability aid only; helps clients pick a matching certificate. Does not change authentication outcomes.

operations.title: Change SSL support method
operations.summary: Choose how this distribution serves HTTPS; dedicating it a fixed IP address costs extra versus the shared default.
operations.description: Set `viewer_certificate.ssl_support_method`, which selects how CloudFront serves HTTPS to viewers. Applicable only when a custom ACM/IAM certificate is configured. Guardrailed because vip and static-ip dedicate IP addresses and incur additional CloudFront charges.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: ssl_support_method
params.label: SSL support method
params.help: sni-only serves HTTPS via SNI at no extra cost; vip and static-ip dedicate IP addresses and incur additional CloudFront charges.

operations.title: Toggle staging flag
operations.summary: Mark this distribution as a staging copy or not; applying REPLACES it - new identity and possible service interruption. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set the `staging` boolean on the distribution (whether it is a staging distribution used for continuous deployment). Provider default is false. On an already-live distribution this can change whether it serves public traffic, so an engineer authors and reviews the change.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: staging
params.label: Staging distribution
params.help: Provider default is false. Flipping this on a live distribution can stop it from serving public traffic.

operations.title: Toggle retain-on-delete
operations.summary: Control whether a future delete disables this distribution instead of destroying it; turning off this safeguard is engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set the `retain_on_delete` boolean, which controls whether a future Terraform destroy DISABLES the distribution instead of deleting it. Provider default is false. Setting it to false removes a data-loss safety guardrail, so an engineer authors and reviews the change. Changing this value has no effect on the currently-served content.

params.name: distribution
params.label: Distribution
params.help: Select the distribution to update.

params.name: retain_on_delete
params.label: Retain on delete
params.help: Provider default is false. true = a future Terraform destroy disables (rather than deletes) the distribution.

operations.title: Provision a new CloudFront distribution
operations.summary: Request a brand-new distribution in front of one origin; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new distribution as a complete specification: one origin, its default cache behavior, viewer certificate, and optional WAF association. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: distribution_name
params.label: Distribution name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the distribution in the generated draft — CloudFront itself has no name field.

params.name: comment
params.label: Comment
params.help: Human-readable description, up to 128 characters.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Enabled
params.help: Whether the distribution serves traffic once created.

params.name: origin_domain_name
params.label: Origin domain name
params.help: The origin's hostname, e.g. the load balancer's DNS name or a static site's endpoint.

params.name: origin_id
params.label: Origin ID
params.help: Internal wiring label linking the origin to the default cache behavior — fixed, never asked.

params.name: origin_protocol_policy
params.label: Origin protocol policy
params.help: How CloudFront talks to the origin. https-only is the secure default.

params.name: origin_http_port
params.label: Origin HTTP port
params.help: Standard HTTP port — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: origin_https_port
params.label: Origin HTTPS port
params.help: Standard HTTPS port — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: origin_ssl_protocols
params.label: Origin SSL protocols
params.help: Modern TLS only — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: target_origin_id
params.label: Target origin ID
params.help: Matches the origin above — fixed, never asked.

params.name: viewer_protocol_policy
params.label: Viewer protocol policy
params.help: allow-all permits insecure HTTP; redirect-to-https or https-only are the secure choices.

params.name: allowed_methods
params.label: Allowed methods
params.help: Read-only methods for the common cacheable-content case — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: cached_methods
params.label: Cached methods
params.help: Matches the allowed methods above — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: compress
params.label: Compress objects automatically
params.help: Whether CloudFront compresses eligible file types.

params.name: cache_policy_id
params.label: Cache policy
params.help: Which AWS-managed or custom cache policy this behavior uses (e.g. CachingOptimized) — an engineer fills in the policy ID during review.

params.name: web_acl
params.label: WAF web ACL
params.help: Leave blank for no firewall; pick an existing CLOUDFRONT-scope web ACL to protect this distribution.

params.name: viewer_certificate_acm
params.label: Custom domain certificate
params.help: Leave blank to use the free default CloudFront certificate (*.cloudfront.net). Pick a certificate to serve a custom domain.

params.name: viewer_certificate_default
params.label: Use default CloudFront certificate
params.help: Set automatically when no custom certificate is chosen above — never asked directly.

params.name: ssl_support_method
params.label: SSL support method
params.help: SNI at no extra cost — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: minimum_protocol_version
params.label: Minimum TLS protocol version
params.help: The current recommended baseline — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: geo_restriction_type
params.label: Geo restriction
params.help: No country blocking for the common case — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Distribution name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this distribution serves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this distribution.

decisions: Cache policy choice (AWS-managed or custom) depends on the origin's content type
Additional ordered cache behaviors for extra path patterns are an engineer decision after creation
Multiple origins, origin groups (failover), and origin access control for private S3 origins are engineer decisions
Custom error responses, access/connection logging, and geo-restriction beyond none are engineer follow-ups
Field-level encryption, real-time logs, and function associations (Lambda@Edge, CloudFront Functions) are engineer decisions
